steps.title: Set up and solve the inequality
steps.content:
We need at least 50 dinner rolls. If we buy $n$ packages with 12 rolls each:
$$12n \geq 50$$
Dividing both sides by 12:
$$n \geq \frac{50}{12} = \frac{25}{6} \approx 4.167$$

steps.title: Round up to the minimum whole number
steps.content:
Since we cannot buy a fractional package, we must round UP. Testing:
  • 4 packages: $12 \times 4 = 48$ rolls (insufficient, since $48 < 50$)
  • 5 packages: $12 \times 5 = 60$ rolls (sufficient, since $60 \geq 50$)
The minimum number of packages is 5.
